behavior_summary: This script provides logic for a left-leg body-part object. When the object is 'used' (or the Avatar/actor enters its trigger region) it marks the related actor/process with a specific info flag and process-exclude state, then spawns a left-leg body-part process (BODYPART::0270) attached to that actor.

When the leg is hit, the script marks the actor/process with a different info flag and process-exclude state, invokes a cleanup/free operation (FREE::2A56 with code 0x0202), queries how many processes of a specific type exist (Kernel::getNumProcesses for type 0x020A) and, depending on that query result, may spawn an additional body-part process (BODYPART::0431).
All spawn return values are captured into a small local (temp) before the handler returns. No global flags are read or written by the documented handlers in this file.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LEGLEFT::LEGLEFT::use (Event 1) executes unconditionally when triggered and always performs: set info (0x0207) -> process exclude -> spawn BODYPART::0270 -> store spawn retval -> return.

U8P_LEGLEFT::LEGLEFT::hit (Event 5) executes on hit: set info (0x020B) -> process exclude -> call FREE::2A56(0x0202, actor) -> call Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x020A) -> test result with a NOT+JNE pattern -> conditionally spawn BODYPART::0431 -> store spawn retval -> return.
The conditional in hit depends on the numeric result of Kernel::getNumProcesses; the disassembly pattern makes the polarity uncertain (hence the spawn-on-zero vs spawn-on-nonzero ambiguity).
open_questions: Precise semantics of the numeric info codes used with 'set info' (0x0207 in use and 0x020B in hit): what concrete state or gameplay meaning do these codes represent?

Exact intended polarity of the Kernel::getNumProcesses check in U8P_LEGLEFT::LEGLEFT::hit (does the handler spawn BODYPART::0431 when zero processes of 0x020A exist, or when any exist?): the disassembly uses a NOT+JNE pattern making polarity ambiguous.
Meaning and gameplay effect of the process type 0x020A queried by Kernel::getNumProcesses (what class/type of processes are being counted?).
Precise effect of FREE::2A56 with parameter 0x0202 on the actor: inferred to be detach/cleanup but the exact resource/state changed is not present in this excerpt.

main_function.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::use (Event 1)
main_function.purpose: Handle the object's 'use' / trigger-area entry: mark the actor/process (set info 0x0207 + process exclude) and spawn the left-leg body-part process BODYPART::0270 attached to that actor.
entry_conditions: Called when the object is used or when the Avatar/actor enters the object's trigger region (Event 1).
No additional runtime gating observed — spawn is unconditional once the event is invoked.

function_map.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::use (Event 1)
function_map.role: Trigger handler that marks the actor/process and spawns the standard left-leg body-part (BODYPART::0270).

function_map.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::hit (Event 5)
function_map.role: Hit/damage handler for the leg: marks actor/process, performs a free/cleanup, queries process counts, and conditionally spawns an alternate body-part (BODYPART::0431).

function_map.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::set info (engine opcode 0x77)
function_map.role: Engine opcode used to set an info/state key on the referenced process/object (used with codes 0x0207 and 0x020B).

function_map.name: U8P_LEGLEFT::process exclude (engine opcode 0x78)
function_map.role: Engine opcode that excludes the marked process from normal processing; used after set info in both handlers.

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: External cleanup/free routine invoked from hit with code 0x0202; likely detaches or releases a linked resource related to the leg.

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Kernel query used by hit to count processes of a specific type (0x020A) to decide conditional spawning.

function_map.name: spawn (BODYPART::0270 / BODYPART::0431)
function_map.role: Engine spawn operation to create/attach body-part processes to the actor; BODYPART::0270 is spawned unconditionally by use, BODYPART::0431 is spawned conditionally by hit.

external_calls.purpose: Annotate/mark the actor/process with info code 0x0207 prior to excluding it from processing.
Exclude the actor/process from normal processing after marking it (paired with set info).
Create the left-leg body-part process instance attached to the actor; return value stored in a local temp.
Annotate/mark the actor/process with info code 0x020B on hit.
Exclude the actor/process from further processing after marking on hit.
Perform cleanup/detach action using code 0x0202 against the actor/process (likely freeing associated resources).
Count processes of type 0x020A to decide whether to spawn BODYPART::0431.
Conditionally create an alternate body-part instance depending on Kernel::getNumProcesses' result; spawn retval stored to a local before return.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script provides logic for a left-leg body-part object. When the object is 'used' (or the Avatar/actor enters its trigger region) it marks the related actor/process with a specific info flag and process-exclude state, then spawns a left-leg body-part process (BODYPART::0270) attached to that actor.
